All Categories

Authors RAVINDER CHAUDHRY B.Sc., D.P.Ed (Gold Medalist) M.P.Ed (Gold Medal with distinction) UGC N.E.T. QUALIFIED Associate Professor Dept. of Physical Education D.A.V. College, CHANDIGARH Dr. SONIA KANWAR M.A. (Phy. Edu.), Ph.D UGC N.E.T. QUALIFIED Assistant Professor Dept. of Physical Education SGGS College, CHANDIGARH HARPREET KAUR B.P.Ed, M.A. (Phy. Edu.), UGC N.E.T. QUALIFIED

PREFACE The present attempt of writing this textbook for B.A. (General) Ist year semester 1 and 2 as per the syllabus issued by Panjab University, Chandigarh was motivated by the desire shared by some of the professionals to update and improve the quality of content. This edition has been introduced to include inspiration with a view to refine the existing content. It is hoped that this book will be found useful by the students. We have tried to present this book in the simplest and easiest vocabulary that caters to the needs of all categories of students. Therefore, the entire book is compartmentalized into two semesters and each semester includes five units on the basis of latest syllabus. We have given quotations, pictorial representations to support and explain our views. This book would expose the readers not only to the basic concept of Physical Education but also impart further knowledge about related concepts. In spite of great care some errors might have crept in, so we request all who have been engaged in different segments of the field to provide us valuable feedback and constructive criticism, so as to make further improvement in this effort by the authors. Last but not the least, we want to express our sincere thanks to our teachers for extending their moral support and encouragement. We are pleased to convey our deep sense of gratitude to Vishvas Publications Pvt. Ltd. for their untiring efforts to bring this book well in time. Authors PUBLISHED BY H.O. : 576, INDUSTRIAL AREA, PHASE-9, MOHALI-160062 (Chandigarh) PHONES : 0172-2210596, 2215045, 502

Top 10 basketball players in india

Best basketball players

Basketball is a team sports invented by James Naismith. It's a very popular game in the United States of America and Philippines, Australia, Iraq and many other countries of the world. A basketball team has 12 players in all but only 5 players play in the court. Basketball players develop coordination, speed, agility and stamina as this sport need a lot of coordination and team work among the players. Basketball is also very famous in India especially in schools and colleges. Throughout the year many tournaments are held at school and college levels. Basketball courts are found in almost all schools and colleges. Many players in India have made their mark in this sport. The achievements of some of these players are sure to impress you and inspire you.

1.Satnam Singh Bhamara

Satnam Singh is an Indian professional wrestler and basketball player.

He was selected by Dallas Mavericks and he became the first Indian player to be drafted into National Basketball Association (NBA). He played at center position. He played high school basketball at IMG Academy in Florida.

Personal information

Born: 10th December 1995, Punjab, India

Playing career: 2015 – 2019

Position: Center

Height: 7 ft 2 in (2.18m)

Weight: 163 kg (360 lb)

Career history

Singh debuted in the year 2011 for senior Indian Nation team at FIBA Asian Championship in Wuhan, China.

In 2017 he played for the National team in FIBA Asia Cup in Beirut, Lebanon

In 2019 he joined the National team for FIBA World Cup qualification.

Current playing team: Dalas Mavericks (Center)

2.Amjyot Singh

Amjyot Singh is an Indian professional Basketball player. He has played for Tokyo Excellence of the National Basketball Development League. This compilation of films covers all sports activities. Sports films have been made since the era of silent films, such as the 1915 film The Champion starring Charlie Chaplin. Films in this genre can range from serious (Raging Bull) to silly (Horse Feathers). A classic theme for sports films is the triumph of an individual or team who prevail despite the difficulties, standard elements of melodrama.
